session.user: Load and follow the skill instructions in .claude/skills/pr-feedback-classifier/SKILL.md Arguments: --pr 9024 Return the complete JSON output as your final message.
session.assistant: I'll start by loading the skill instructions and then execute it with the specified argument.
session.tool_result:
1→--- 2→name: pr-feedback-classifier 3→description: > 4→ Fetches and classifies PR review feedback with context isolation. 5→ Returns structured JSON with thread IDs for deterministic resolution. 6→ Use when analyzing PR comments before addressing them. 7→argument-hint: "[--pr <number>] [--include-resolved]" 8→context: fork 9→agent: general-purpose 10→model: sonnet 11→--- 12→ 13→# PR Feedback Classifier 14→ 15→Fetch and classify all PR review feedback for the current branch's PR. 16→ 17→## Arguments 18→ 19→- `--pr <number>`: Target a specific PR by number (default: current branch's PR) 20→- `--include-resolved`: Include resolved threads (for reference) 21→ 22→Check `$ARGUMENTS` for flags. 23→ 24→## Critical Constraints 25→ 26→**DO NOT write Python scripts or any code files.** Classify the data using direct AI reasoning only. Writing code to process JSON is unnecessary and pollutes the filesystem. 27→ 28→## Steps 29→ 30→1. **Fetch PR info and all comments in a single call:**
... [194 lines omitted] ...
130→ "action_summary": "Bot suggestion: add unit tests for error handling paths", 132→ "original_comment": "Consider adding unit tests for the error handling paths in this endpoint" 170→ "error": null 184→## Error Case 198→ "error": "No PR found for branch feature-xyz" 216→ "error": null
session.assistant: Now I'll execute the skill with the specified PR argument:
session.tool_result:
{ "success": true, "pr_number": 9024, "pr_url": "https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/pull/9024", "pr_title": "Add dynamic validation test for @json_command output_types against return annotations", "review_threads": [], "discussion_comments": [ { "id": 4019778209, "author": "schrockn", "body": "🚀 Starting implementation --- **Worktree:** `erk-slot-19` **Branch:** `plnd/test-output-types-match-03-08-1452`", "url": "https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/pull/9024#issuecomment-4019778209" }, { "id": 4019789456, "author": "schrockn", "body": "> [!WARNING] > This pull request is not mergeable via GitHub because a downstack PR is open. Once all requirements are satisfied, merge this PR as a stack on Graphite. > Learn more * **#9029** * **#9024** 👈 (View in Graphite) * **#9022** * **#9013** * `master`

This stack of pull requests is managed by Graphite. Learn more about stacking. ", "url": "https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/pull/9024#issuecomment-4019789456" }, { "id": 4019791408, "author": "github-actions[bot]", "body": " ## ✅ Test Coverage Review **Last updated:** 2026-03-08 12:04:03 PT No coverage violations detected. All source modifications have corresponding test coverage.
Details ### Source Files | File | Status | Tests | |------|--------|-------| | `src/erk/cli/json_command.py` | Modified | ✅ `tests/unit/cli/test_json_command.py` | ### Files Reviewed - `src/erk/cli/json_command.py`: 0 violations - `tests/unit/cli/test_json_command.py`: New test function added --- ### Activity Log - **2026-03-08 12:04:03 PT**: Source modification to enable callback introspection is covered by new validation test
", "url": "https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/pull/9024#issuecomment-4019791408" }, { "id": 4019791455, "author": "github-actions[bot]", "body": " ## ✅ Dignified Code Simplifier Review **Last updated:** 2026-03-08 12:04:04 PT Found 0 violations. All code adheres to dignified-python standards.
Details ### Patterns Checked ✅ LBYL vs EAFP - All checks use LBYL pattern correctly ✅ Type hints - Proper use of typing module APIs ✅ Code clarity - Helper functions are simple and focused ✅ Assertions - Descriptive error messages provided ### Files Reviewed - `src/erk/cli/json_command.py`: 0 violations - `tests/unit/cli/test_json_command.py`: 0 violations --- ### Activity Log - **2026-03-08 12:04:04 PT**: No violations detected - code follows all dignified-python standards
", "url": "https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/pull/9024#issuecomment-4019791455" ... [15 lines omitted] ... "body": " ## ❌ Dignified Python Review **Last updated:** 2026-03-08 12:04:21 PT Found 1 violation across 1 file. Inline comment posted.
Details ### Patterns Checked ✅ LBYL exception handling - None found ✅ Path operations - None found ✅ Frozen dataclasses - None found ✅ Subprocess handling - None found ✅ Default parameters - None found ✅ Import aliases - None found ❌ Inline imports - Found in tests/unit/cli/test_json_command.py:523 ### Violations Summary - `test_json_command.py:523`: Redundant inline import of 'click' already available at module level ### Files Reviewed - `src/erk/cli/json_command.py`: 0 violations - `tests/unit/cli/test_json_command.py`: 1 violation --- ### Activity Log - **2026-03-08 12:04:21 PT**: Found 1 violation (redundant inline import in test_json_command.py:523)
",

session.assistant:
Now I'll analyze the feedback and classify it according to the skill's instructions:
The PR 9024 has: - **0 review threads** (no code review comments) - **5 discussion comments** from bots and the author: 1. Implementation start notification (informational) 2. Graphite stack warning (informational) 3. Test coverage review bot - No violations (informational) 4. Code simplifier bot - No violations (informational) 5. Dignified Python bot - **1 violation** found (actionable) 6. Tripwires review bot - No violations (informational)
The dignified-python-review found 1 violation: - **File**: `tests/unit/cli/test_json_command.py` - **Line**: 523 - **Issue**: Redundant inline import of 'click' already available at module level
The git diff shows no file restructuring (renames, moves, copies) - only modifications and additions. The violation is from a bot detecting an inline import issue in a test file.
Based on the classification rules: - The violation is from a bot (`github-actions[bot]`) - However, there's **no file restructuring** in this PR (no renames/moves detected) - Therefore, `pre_existing` = false - The issue is a single-line inline import that should be removed - **Complexity**: `local`
```json { "success": true, "pr_number": 9024, "pr_title": "Add dynamic validation test for @json_command output_types against return annotations", "pr_url": "https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/pull/9024", "actionable_threads": [ { "thread_id": "discussion_4019791932", "type": "discussion", "path": "tests/unit/cli/test_json_command.py", "line": 523, "is_outdated": false, "classification": "actionable", "pre_existing": false, "action_summary": "Remove redundant inline import of 'click' - already available at module level", "complexity": "local", "original_comment": "Redundant inline import of 'click' already available at module level" } ], "discussion_actions": [], "informational_count": 5, "batches": [ { "name": "Pre-Existing (Auto-Resolve)", "complexity": "pre_existing", "auto_proceed": true, "item_indices": [] }, { "name": "Local Fixes", "complexity": "local", "auto_proceed": true, "item_indices": [0] }, { "name": "Single-File", "complexity": "single_file", "auto_proceed": true, "item_indices": [] }, { "name": "Cross-Cutting", "complexity": "cross_cutting", "auto_proceed": false, "item_indices": [] }, { "name": "Complex", "complexity": "complex", "auto_proceed": false, "item_indices": [] }, { "name": "Informational", "complexity": "informational", "auto_proceed": false, "item_indices": [] } ], "error": null } ```
